message.content:
You are executing a bridled pace for the Job Jockey system. Your task is to implement the changes described below precisely.

## Project Context

This is a Rust project. Key build commands:
- Build: `./tt/vow-b.Build.sh`
- Test: `./tt/vow-t.Test.sh`

Never `cd` in Bash commands — use absolute paths or run from project root.
When running cargo directly, use `--manifest-path` to stay at project root.

## Pace: add-vvx-invitatory (₢AWAAO)

## Docket (what to accomplish)

Create vvx invitatory command in VVC: officium gap check + probe + vvb:...:i: commit.

Terminology: "officium" (Latin: the Divine Office) replaces "session" for the bounded work period concept. The invitatory opens a new officium.

### Gap detection: vvcp_needs_officium

New function in Tools/vvc/src/vvcp_probe.rs. Logic: scan git log for most recent vvb:...:i: commit. If none found or gap >1 hour, return true. No backward compat with old jjb:...:s: commits.

RCG compliance: Build the grep pattern from const strings, not magic text. Use VVCC_BRAND_PREFIX (from ₢AWAAN) and a new const for the invitatory action code (e.g., VVCC_ACTION_INVITATORY: &str = "i"). Pattern assembled as format!("^{}:.*:{}:", VVCC_BRAND_PREFIX, VVCC_ACTION_INVITATORY). Pass to git log --all --grep=<pattern> --format="%ai" -1.

### CLI registration

Register "invitatory" as a new Commands variant in Tools/vok/src/vorm_main.rs (alongside VvxCommit, VvxGuard, etc.). The handler calls vvc::invitatory() — a thin delegation, same pattern as existing VvxCommit → vvc::commit().

### VVC async export

Export pub async fn vvcc_invitatory() from VVC (in vvcp_probe.rs or vvcc_format.rs). This is the function that both the CLI handler AND JJK (in ₢AWAAP) call directly via Rust async — no subprocess. VVC already has tokio in Cargo.toml.

The invitatory function:
a. Calls vvcp_needs_officium() — if not needed, print "Officium current", return Ok
b. Calls vvcp_probe() for model IDs
c. Gets hallmark via vvcc_get_hallmark() (from ₢AWAAN)
d. Formats commit: vvcc_format_branded(VVCC_BRAND_PREFIX, hallmark, "", VVCC_ACTION_INVITATORY, "OFFICIUM {timestamp}", Some(probe_data))
e. Creates empty commit via vvc::commit() with allow_empty=true, no_stage=true
f. Prints "Officium: {commit_hash} ({timestamp})" on success

RCG compliance: "OFFICIUM" in the subject should be a const (e.g., VVCC_OFFICIUM_TOKEN). The timestamp format should reference or derive from existing timestamp consts if any.

### VOS spec

Add to Tools/vok/vov_veiled/VOS-VoxObscuraSpec.adoc:
- voscai_invitatory action code i (lowercase) in Commit Message Architecture section
- Definition references "officium" as the concept being opened
- Example: vvb:1011::i: OFFICIUM 260207-0701

### Testing

Build and test: tt/vow-b.Build.sh && tt/vow-t.Test.sh
Manual test: lower gap threshold temporarily, run vvx invitatory, verify commit format with git log -1.

### Key files
- Tools/vvc/src/vvcp_probe.rs (add vvcp_needs_officium, vvcc_invitatory)
- Tools/vvc/src/lib.rs (export invitatory)
- Tools/vok/src/vorm_main.rs (register Commands::Invitatory, thin handler)
- Tools/vok/vov_veiled/VOS-VoxObscuraSpec.adoc (add voscai_invitatory, define officium)

## Warrant (how to execute)

Steps:
1. In vvcp_probe.rs add const VVCP_OFFICIUM_GAP_SECS: u64 = 3600 and const VVCC_ACTION_INVITATORY: &str = "i" and const VVCC_OFFICIUM_TOKEN: &str = "OFFICIUM"
2. In vvcp_probe.rs add fn vvcp_needs_officium that builds grep pattern from vvc::VVCC_BRAND_PREFIX and VVCC_ACTION_INVITATORY using format, runs git log --all --grep=pattern --format=%ai -1, parses timestamp, returns true if none found or gap exceeds VVCP_OFFICIUM_GAP_SECS
3. In vvcp_probe.rs add pub async fn vvcc_invitatory that calls vvcp_needs_officium and if not needed prints "Officium current" and returns Ok, otherwise calls vvcp_probe for model IDs, calls vvc::vvcc_get_hallmark for hallmark, formats commit via vvc::vvcc_format_branded with VVCC_BRAND_PREFIX as brand and hallmark and empty string identity and VVCC_ACTION_INVITATORY action and VVCC_OFFICIUM_TOKEN plus space plus YYMMDD-HHMM timestamp as subject and probe_data as body, creates empty commit via vvc::commit with allow_empty=true and no_stage=true, prints "Officium: commit_hash timestamp" on success
4. In lib.rs re-export vvcc_invitatory and VVCC_ACTION_INVITATORY
5. In vorm_main.rs register Commands::Invitatory variant with command name "vvx_invitatory", add run_invitatory async handler that calls vvc::vvcc_invitatory().await, wire into main match arm
6. In VOS-VoxObscuraSpec.adoc add linked term vost_officium with anchor and attribute definition as a bounded work period opened by the invitatory, then add voscai_invitatory action code "i" in Action Codes section after voscar_release following same pattern as existing action codes, referencing vost_officium, with example vvb:1011::i: OFFICIUM 260207-0701

Verify: ./tt/vow-b.Build.sh && ./tt/vow-t.Test.sh

## Wrap Discipline

DO NOT run /jjc-pace-wrap or mark the pace complete. When work is done:
1. Run build + tests as specified in direction
2. Report completion status and test results
3. STOP — the calling agent will confirm wrap with the user

   182→
   183→== Overview
   184→
   185→The Vox Obscura Specification defines the release and installation infrastructure for Claude Code
   186→{vose_kit_s}.
   187→{vose_kit_s}
   188→are developed in a
   189→{vosp_kit_forge}
   190→and distributed via
   191→{vose_parcel_s}
   192→to
   193→{vosp_target_repo}
   194→consumers.
   195→
   196→Each
   197→{vose_kit}
   198→declares itself to the
   199→{vose_conclave}
   200→through a
   201→{vose_whisper}
   202→- a builder-pattern API that registers the
   203→{vose_kit_p}
   204→identity,
   205→{vose_managed_section_s},
   206→and
   207→{vosa_concept_model_s}.
   208→
   209→The
   210→{vosor_release}
   211→operation creates a
   212→{vose_parcel}
   213→containing all
   214→{vose_kit}
   215→assets.
   216→The
   217→{vosoi_install}
   218→operation deploys a
   219→{vose_parcel}
   220→to a
   221→{vosp_target_repo}.
   222→The
   223→{vosou_uninstall}
   224→operation removes Claude Code integration (commands, hooks, CLAUDE.md sections, vvx binary) while preserving
   225→{vose_kit}
   226→directories.
   227→Uses
   228→{vosls_signet}
   229→-based prefix matching for command/hook removal.
   230→
   231→**Related Specifications:**
   232→
   233→* **VLS-VoxLiturgicalSpec.adoc** — Universal naming vocabulary (Cipher, Signet, Epithet, Inscription, Vesture)
   234→* **BUS-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc** — Tabtarget dispatch vocabulary (Colophon, Frontispiece, Imprint, Formulary, Launcher)
   235→
   236→== Key Premises
   237→
   238→[[vosk_prefix_validation]]
   239→// ⟦axl_voices axk_premise⟧
   240→{vosk_prefix_validation}::
   241→All
   242→{vosa_command_s},
   243→{vosa_hook_s},
   244→{vosa_skill_s},
   245→and
   246→{vosa_subagent_s}
   247→must have
   248→{vosls_signet_s}
   249→derived from their
   250→{vose_kit_p}
   251→{voslc_cipher}.
   252→{vosor_release}
   253→fails if any asset's
   254→{vosli_inscription}
   255→violates the
   256→{voslc_cipher}
   257→derivation rules.
   258→This constraint enables deterministic
   259→{vosou_uninstall}
   260→by
   261→{vosls_signet}
   262→matching.
   263→+
   264→Validation applies to artifact *file names* only.
   265→Internal content (function names, variable names within scripts) is not validated during
   266→{vosor_release}.
   267→
   268→[[vosk_all_or_none]]
   269→// ⟦axl_voices axk_premise⟧
   270→{vosk_all_or_none}::
   271→{vosoi_install}
   272→is atomic.
   273→A
   274→{vose_parcel}
   275→represents a tested, coherent release.
   276→No selective installation of individual
   277→{vose_kit_s}
   278→or assets.
   279→
   280→[[vosk_parcel_self_contained]]
   281→// ⟦axl_voices axk_premise⟧
   282→{vosk_parcel_self_contained}::
   283→A
   284→{vose_parcel}
   285→contains everything needed for installation.
   286→No dependency resolution or external fetches during
   287→{vosoi_install}.
   288→The
   289→{vose_parcel}
   290→can function from its extracted directory.
   291→
   292→[[vosk_burc_prerequisite]]
   293→// ⟦axl_voices axk_premise⟧
   294→{vosk_burc_prerequisite}::
   295→{vosoi_install}
   296→requires BURC (BUK Regime Configuration) in the
   297→{vosp_target_repo}.
   298→The install script reads `.buk/burc.env` for required variables:
   299→`BURC_PROJECT_ROOT` (path from burc.env's location to project root),
   300→`BURC_TOOLS_DIR` (relative to project root), and
   301→`BURC_MANAGED_KITS` (comma-separated list of kits included in vvx).
   302→Install fails immediately if any required variable is missing — no bootstrap, no prompts.
   303→
   304→[[vosk_default_routing]]
   305→// ⟦axl_voices axk_premise⟧
   306→{vosk_default_routing}::
   307→Assets without special routing rules copy in place, preserving directory structure relative to
   308→{vose_kit}
   309→root.
   310→Only
   311→{vosa_command_s}
   312→and
   313→{vosa_hook_s}
   314→have special routing (to `.claude/commands/` and `.claude/hooks/` respectively).
   315→All other files install to `${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/{kit}/` maintaining their source-relative paths.
   316→
   317→== Control Terms
   318→
   319→VOS control terms voice `axc_*` motifs for bash scripted execution context.
   320→These terms appear inline within procedure steps to classify actions.
   321→
   322→[[vosc_require]]
   323→// ⟦axl_voices axc_require⟧
   324→{vosc_require}::
   325→Validation check that confirms an expected condition.
   326→Script exits with error if condition not met.
   327→
   328→[[vosc_fatal]]
   329→// ⟦axl_voices axc_fatal⟧
   330→{vosc_fatal}::
   331→Immediate termination with error status.
   332→Displays error message and exits non-zero.
   333→
   334→[[vosc_store]]
   335→// ⟦axl_voices axc_store⟧
   336→{vosc_store}::
   337→Capture a value into a shell variable for later use.
   338→
   339→[[vosc_call]]
   340→// ⟦axl_voices axc_call⟧
   341→{vosc_call}::
   342→Invoke an external command or function.
   343→Captures exit status for processing.
   344→
   345→[[vosc_show]]
   346→// ⟦axl_voices axc_show⟧
   347→{vosc_show}::
   348→Display information to stdout.
   349→Progress messages or results.
   350→
   351→[[vosc_step]]
   352→// ⟦axl_voices axc_step⟧
   353→{vosc_step}::
   354→Progress message indicating completion of a processing stage.
   355→
   356→== Section Headers
   357→
   358→VOS section headers voice `axs_*` motifs for procedure documentation structure.
   359→
   360→[[voss_inputs]]
   361→// ⟦axl_voices axs_inputs⟧
   362→{voss_inputs}::
   363→Section header for operation input parameters.
   364→
   365→[[voss_behavior]]
   366→// ⟦axl_voices axs_behavior⟧
   367→{voss_behavior}::
   368→Section header for operation behavior steps.
   369→
   370→[[voss_outputs]]
   371→// ⟦axl_voices axs_outputs⟧
   372→{voss_outputs}::
   373→Section header for operation outputs and artifacts.
   374→
   375→[[voss_completion]]
   376→// ⟦axl_voices axs_completion⟧
   377→{voss_completion}::
   378→Section header for operation completion criteria.
   379→
   380→== Types
   381→
   382→[[vost_hallmark]]
   383→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   384→{vost_hallmark}::
   385→A 4-digit version identifier starting at 1000.
   386→{vost_hallmark_s}
   387→increment for each unique content set during
   388→{vosor_release}.
   389→Unlike
   390→{vost_sigil}
   391→(timestamp-based),
   392→{vost_hallmark}
   393→represents content identity — same content always produces same
   394→{vost_hallmark}.
   395→+
   396→Examples: `1000`, `1001`, `1042`.
   397→
   398→[[vost_sigil]]
   399→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   400→{vost_sigil}::
   401→A version identifier in YYMMDD-HHMM format.
   402→Generated at
   403→{vosor_release}
   404→time.
   405→Appears in
   406→{vose_parcel}
   407→filenames and manifests.
   408→+
   409→Example: `260115-1430` for January 15, 2026 at 14:30.

   411→== Entities
   412→
   413→[[vose_parcel]]
   414→// ⟦axl_voices axo_entity⟧
   415→{vose_parcel}::
   416→The distribution archive for
   417→{vose_kit_s}.
   418→Named `vvk-parcel-{hallmark}.tar.gz`.
   419→Contains platform binaries and plain-text
   420→{vose_kit}
   421→assets.
   422→+
   423→Structure:
   424→+
   425→----
   426→vvk-parcel-1000/
   427→  vvi_install.sh          # Install script (BCG-style, not installed)
   428→  vvbf_brand.json         # Brand file (runtime identity)
   429→  kits/
   430→    buk/
   431→      buc_command.sh
   432→      ...
   433→    jjk/
   434→      jju_utility.sh
   435→      ...
   436→    vvk/
   437→      bin/
   438→        vvx-darwin-arm64
   439→        vvx-darwin-x86_64
   440→        vvx-linux-x86_64
   441→      vvu_uninstall.sh
   442→  claude/
   443→    commands/
   444→      jjc-heat-mount.md
   445→      cma-normalize.md
   446→      vvc-commit.md
   447→    hooks/                # Future
   448→----
   449→+
   450→The `kits/` directory mirrors the target `Tools/` structure: `kits/*` becomes `Tools/*`.
   451→The `claude/` directory contains Claude Code configuration assets collected from kit forges and routed to `.claude/*` in the target.
   452→VVK is a
   453→{vose_kit}
   454→like any other — it just happens to contain binaries.
   455→+
   456→**Scripts:**
   457→+
   458→* `vvi_install.sh` — Bootstrap script at archive root. Takes `burc.env` path as argument; Rust validates file and parses needed variables (`BURC_TOOLS_DIR`, `BURC_PROJECT_ROOT`). Not installed to target.
   459→* `vvu_uninstall.sh` — Lives in `kits/vvk/`, installs to `${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/vvk/`.
   460→
   461→[[vose_whisper]]
   462→// ⟦axl_voices axo_entity⟧
   463→{vose_whisper}::
   464→A
   465→{vose_kit_p}
   466→declaration to the
   467→{vose_conclave}.
   468→Implemented as a Rust builder API in the
   469→{vose_kit_p}
   470→{vosp_veiled}
   471→directory.
   472→+
   473→Builder API:
   474→+
   475→[source,rust]
   476→----
   477→use voci_ciphers::JJ;
   478→
   479→Whisper::for_kit("jjk")
   480→    .cipher(JJ)                 // symbolic reference to const
   481→    .display_name("Job Jockey")
   482→    .managed_section("vocjjmc_core.md", "JJK")
   483→    .managed_section("vocjjmc_gallops.md", "JJK_GALLOPS")
   484→    .concept_model("JJSA-GallopsData.adoc")
   485→    .register(&mut conclave)
   486→----
   487→+
   488→File location: `Tools/{kit}/vov_veiled/vo{cipher}w_whisper.rs`
   489→
   490→[[vose_conclave]]
   491→// ⟦axl_voices axo_entity⟧
   492→{vose_conclave}::
   493→The central API that
   494→{vose_kit_s}
   495→use to describe themselves.
   496→Lives in VOK as `vocv_conclave.rs`.
   497→Collects
   498→{vose_whisper_s}
   499→and validates
   500→{vosk_prefix_validation}
   501→during
   502→{vosor_release}.
   503→+
   504→Responsibilities:
   505→+
   506→* Collect
   507→{vose_whisper_s}
   508→from all participating
   509→{vose_kit_s}
   510→* Enforce
   511→{vosk_prefix_validation}
   512→during
   513→{vosor_release}
   514→* Provide asset enumeration for
   515→{vosoi_install}
   516→and
   517→{vosou_uninstall}
   518→
   519→[[vose_kit]]
   520→// ⟦axl_voices axo_entity⟧
   521→{vose_kit}::
   522→A distributable Claude Code extension.
   523→Lives in `Tools/{kit}/` in the
   524→{vosp_kit_forge}.
   525→+
   526→Structure:
   527→+
   528→----
   529→Tools/{kit}/
   530→  {kit}a_arcanum.sh      # Public - install script
   531→  {kit}u_utility.sh      # Public - utilities
   532→  README.md              # Public - documentation
   533→  commands/              # Public - slash commands
   534→    {cipher}c-*.md
   535→  vov_veiled/            # Private - never distributed
   536→    vo{cipher}w_whisper.rs
   537→    vo{cipher}mc_*.md    # CLAUDE.md templates
   538→    {ACRONYM}-*.adoc     # Concept models
   539→----
   540→+
   541→**VVK Special Status:**
   542→VVK (Voce Viva Kit) is a
   543→{vose_kit}
   544→with unique characteristics:
   545→+
   546→* Has a
   547→{vose_whisper}
   548→like other kits (for CLAUDE.md knowledge)
   549→* Contents are primarily binaries (`vvx-{platform}`) and the uninstall script
   550→* Installed like any other kit — `kits/vvk/` in
   551→{vose_parcel}
   552→becomes `Tools/vvk/` in target
   553→* Present in every
   554→{vosp_target_repo}
   555→— the universal kit
   556→* Uses cipher `vv` (Voce Viva)
   557→
   558→[[vose_managed_section]]
   559→// ⟦axl_voices axo_entity⟧
   560→{vose_managed_section}::
   561→A block of content in the target CLAUDE.md file bounded by
   562→{vose_marker_s}.
   563→Each
   564→{vose_kit}
   565→may contribute multiple
   566→{vose_managed_section_s}
   567→for distinct feature groups.
   568→+
   569→Members:
   570→
   571→[[vosem_file]]
   572→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   573→* {vosem_file} - Template file path relative to
   574→{vosp_veiled}
   575→(e.g., `vocjjmc_core.md`)
   576→
   577→[[vosem_tag]]
   578→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   579→* {vosem_tag} - Identifier used in
   580→{vose_marker_s}
   581→(e.g., `JJK`, `JJK_GALLOPS`)
   582→
   583→[[vose_marker]]
   584→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   585→{vose_marker}::
   586→Delimiters bounding a
   587→{vose_managed_section}
   588→in CLAUDE.md.
   589→+
   590→Format: `<\!-- MANAGED:{tag}:BEGIN -->` and `<\!-- MANAGED:{tag}:END -->`
   591→+
   592→Content between
   593→{vose_marker_s}
   594→is replaced entirely on
   595→{vosoi_install}.
   596→Content outside
   597→{vose_marker_s}
   598→is preserved.
   599→
   600→[[vose_uninstalled_marker]]
   601→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   602→{vose_uninstalled_marker}::
   603→Placeholder preserving position of a removed
   604→{vose_managed_section}.
   605→+
   606→Format: `<\!-- MANAGED:{tag}:UNINSTALLED -->`
   607→+
   608→On
   609→{vosoi_install},
   610→an
   611→{vose_uninstalled_marker}
   612→is expanded back to
   613→{vose_marker_s}
   614→at the same location.
   615→On
   616→{vosou_uninstall},
   617→{vose_marker_s}
   618→are collapsed to a single
   619→{vose_uninstalled_marker},
   620→preserving user's chosen section ordering for future reinstall.
   621→
   622→[[vose_brand_file]]
   623→// ⟦axl_voices axj_structure⟧
   624→{vose_brand_file}::
   625→Runtime identity file at
   626→{vose_parcel}
   627→root.
   628→Contains
   629→{vost_hallmark},
   630→minted timestamp, and content super-SHA.
   631→+
   632→File location: `vvbf_brand.json` at parcel root.
   633→Installed to `.vvk/vvbf_brand.json` in target.
   634→+
   635→Members:
   636→
   637→[[vosem_hallmark]]
   638→// ⟦axl_voices axj_field axd_required⟧
   639→* {vosem_hallmark} — {axj_field_tag}: `vvbh_hallmark`.
   640→4-digit version identifier (
   641→{vost_hallmark}
   642→).
   643→
   644→[[vosem_brand_date]]
   645→// ⟦axl_voices axj_field axd_required⟧
   646→* {vosem_brand_date} — {axj_field_tag}: `vvbd_date`.
   647→Minted timestamp in YYMMDD-HHMM format.
   648→
   649→[[vosem_brand_sha]]
   650→// ⟦axl_voices axj_field axd_required⟧
   651→* {vosem_brand_sha} — {axj_field_tag}: `vvbs_sha`.
   652→Content super-SHA (order-independent hash of all parcel files).
   653→
   654→[[vosem_brand_commit]]
   655→// ⟦axl_voices axj_field axd_required⟧
   656→* {vosem_brand_commit} — {axj_field_tag}: `vvbc_commit`.
   657→Git commit SHA of source repo at release time.
   658→
   659→[[vosem_brand_kits]]
   660→// ⟦axl_voices axj_field axd_required⟧
   661→* {vosem_brand_kits} — {axj_field_tag}: `vvbk_kits`.
   662→Array of kit identifiers included in this release.
   663→Used by
   664→{vosou_uninstall}
   665→to enumerate installed kits.
   666→
   667→[[vose_registry]]
   668→// ⟦axl_voices axj_structure⟧
   669→{vose_registry}::
   670→{vosp_veiled}
   671→mapping of
   672→{vost_hallmark}
   673→to content identity.
   674→Keys are
   675→{vost_hallmark}
   676→values; entries contain
   677→{vosem_brand_date}
   678→and
   679→{vosem_brand_sha}.
   680→+
   681→File location: `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/vovr_registry.json`
   682→+
   683→Used by
   684→{vosor_release}
   685→to allocate
   686→{vost_hallmark_s}
   687→— if super-SHA exists, reuse its
   688→{vost_hallmark};
   689→otherwise allocate next sequential value.

   829→== Operations
   830→
   831→[[vosor_release]]
   832→// ⟦axl_voices axo_command axe_bash_scripted axd_transient⟧
   833→=== {vosor_release}
   834→
   835→Creates a
   836→{vose_parcel}
   837→from the
   838→{vosp_kit_forge}.
   839→
   840→{voss_inputs}
   841→
   842→// ⟦axd_none⟧
   843→No external inputs. Operates on current
   844→{vosp_kit_forge}
   845→state.
   846→
   847→{voss_behavior}
   848→
   849→. *Collect kit declarations*:
   850→.. {vosc_call}
   851→read `BURC_MANAGED_KITS` from kit forge's `.buk/burc.env`
   852→.. {vosc_fatal}
   853→if missing or empty: "burc.env missing required variable: BURC_MANAGED_KITS"
   854→.. {vosc_store}
   855→kit list for processing (only kits in BURC_MANAGED_KITS)
   856→
   857→. *Validate prefix compliance*:
   858→.. For each
   859→{vose_kit}:
   860→{vosc_require}
   861→all asset
   862→{vosls_signet_s}
   863→derive from
   864→{voslc_cipher}
   865→.. {vosc_fatal}
   866→if any violations: list non-compliant
   867→{vosli_inscription_s}
   868→
   869→. *Run testbenches*:
   870→.. {vosc_call}
   871→each kit's testbench
   872→.. {vosc_fatal}
   873→if any tests fail
   874→
   875→. *Build binaries*:
   876→.. {vosc_call}
   877→cargo build for each target platform
   878→.. {vosc_store}
   879→binary paths
   880→
   881→. *Collect assets*:
   882→.. For each
   883→{vose_kit}:
   884→collect files excluding
   885→{vosp_veiled}
   886→.. For each
   887→{voslc_cipher}
   888→from kit list:
   889→... Scan kit forge `.claude/commands/` for files matching `{cipher}c-*.md`
   890→... Scan kit forge `.claude/hooks/` for files matching `{cipher}h-*.md`
   891→... Copy matching files to parcel `claude/commands/` and `claude/hooks/` respectively
   892→.. {vosc_step}
   893→"Claude config assets collected by cipher pattern"
   894→
   895→. *Generate sigil*:
   896→.. {vosc_store}
   897→{vost_sigil}
   898→as current timestamp (YYMMDD-HHMM)
   899→
   900→. *Compute brand identity*:
   901→.. {vosc_call}
   902→compute order-independent super-SHA:
   903→... Sort all parcel files by path
   904→... Hash each file's path + content
   905→... Combine hashes
   906→... Exclude
   907→{vose_brand_file}
   908→from computation
   909→.. {vosc_store}
   910→super-SHA for registry lookup
   911→
   912→. *Allocate hallmark*:
   913→.. {vosc_call}
   914→read
   915→{vose_registry}
   916→.. If super-SHA exists in registry:
   917→{vosc_store}
   918→existing
   919→{vost_hallmark}
   920→.. If super-SHA is new:
   921→... {vosc_store}
   922→next sequential
   923→{vost_hallmark}
   924→(max + 1, starting at 1000)
   925→... {vosc_call}
   926→update
   927→{vose_registry}
   928→with new entry
   929→... {vosc_call}
   930→commit registry change
   931→
   932→. *Write brand file*:
   933→.. {vosc_call}
   934→create
   935→{vose_brand_file}
   936→at parcel root with
   937→{vosem_hallmark},
   938→{vosem_brand_date},
   939→{vosem_brand_sha},
   940→{vosem_brand_commit}
   941→(current git HEAD),
   942→{vosem_brand_kits}
   943→(list of kit identifiers)
   944→
   945→. *Package parcel*:
   946→.. Create `vvk-parcel-{hallmark}.tar.gz` with structure:
   947→... `vvi_install.sh` — install script (bootstrap, not installed)
   948→... `vvbf_brand.json` —
   949→{vose_brand_file}
   950→at parcel root
   951→... `kits/` — all kit assets by directory (including VVK with binaries)
   952→... `claude/` — commands and hooks collected by cipher pattern
   953→
   954→{voss_outputs}
   955→
   956→* {vose_parcel}
   957→archive: `vvk-parcel-{hallmark}.tar.gz`
   958→* {vose_brand_file}
   959→at parcel root
   960→
   961→{voss_completion}
   962→
   963→{vose_parcel}
   964→created with all validated
   965→{vose_kit}
   966→assets and platform binaries.
   967→
   968→
   969→[[vosoi_install]]
   970→// ⟦axl_voices axo_command axe_rust_impl axd_transient⟧
   971→=== {vosoi_install}
   972→
   973→Deploys a
   974→{vose_parcel}
   975→to a
   976→{vosp_target_repo}.
   977→Nuclear replacement: deletes existing installation before copying fresh.
   978→Handles both first-time install and reinstall identically.
   979→
   980→Invocation (from extracted parcel):
   981→
   982→----
   983→./vvi_install.sh /path/to/target/.buk/burc.env
   984→----
   985→
   986→The bash script is a thin bootstrap: detect platform, exec `vvx_emplace`.
   987→All logic is implemented in Rust for path safety and testability.
   988→
   989→{voss_inputs}
   990→
   991→// ⟦axd_required⟧
   992→* Path to target repository's `burc.env` file (positional argument)
   993→
   994→{voss_behavior}
   995→
   996→. *Validate burc.env*:
   997→.. {vosc_require}
   998→burc.env exists and is readable
   999→.. {vosc_call}
  1000→parse burc.env as KEY=VALUE pairs
  1001→.. {vosc_require}
  1002→`BURC_PROJECT_ROOT` is defined and non-empty
  1003→.. {vosc_require}
  1004→`BURC_TOOLS_DIR` is defined and non-empty
  1005→.. {vosc_require}
  1006→`BURC_MANAGED_KITS` is defined and non-empty
  1007→.. {vosc_fatal}
  1008→if missing or empty: "burc.env missing required variable: {name}"
  1009→.. {vosc_call}
  1010→resolve `BURC_PROJECT_ROOT` as path from burc.env's location to project root
  1011→.. {vosc_call}
  1012→resolve `BURC_TOOLS_DIR` relative to resolved project_root (per BURC spec)
  1013→.. {vosc_store}
  1014→absolute paths for tools_dir and project_root, kit list from BURC_MANAGED_KITS
  1015→
  1016→. *Validate git state*:
  1017→.. {vosc_require}
  1018→project_root is a git repository
  1019→.. {vosc_fatal}
  1020→if not: "Target is not a git repository"
  1021→.. {vosc_require}
  1022→working tree is clean (no uncommitted changes)
  1023→.. {vosc_fatal}
  1024→if dirty: "Target repo has uncommitted changes. Commit or stash before install."
  1025→
  1026→. *Read brand identity*:
  1027→.. {vosc_call}
  1028→parse
  1029→{vose_brand_file}
  1030→from parcel root
  1031→.. {vosc_store}
  1032→{vosem_hallmark}
  1033→and
  1034→{vosem_brand_kits}
  1035→
  1036→. *Validate kit match*:
  1037→.. {vosc_require}
  1038→`BURC_MANAGED_KITS` exactly matches
  1039→{vosem_brand_kits}
  1040→(same kits, order independent)
  1041→.. {vosc_fatal}
  1042→if mismatch: "Target requests kits [{target_kits}] but parcel contains [{parcel_kits}]"
  1043→
  1044→. *Nuclear cleanup*:

  1301→== Commit Message Architecture
  1302→
  1303→VOK operations use a specialized commit message
  1304→{voscf_format}
  1305→that shares the
  1306→{vost_hallmark}
  1307→concept with Job Jockey but omits identity nesting (no heat/pace hierarchy).
  1308→
  1309→[[voscf_format]]
  1310→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
  1311→{voscf_format}::
  1312→The canonical structure for VVK commits:
  1313→+
  1314→----
  1315→vvb:HALLMARK::ACTION: message
  1316→----
  1317→+
  1318→Fields:
  1319→+
  1320→* {vosca_vvb}
  1321→— Voce Viva brand prefix (literal string)
  1322→* `HALLMARK` —
  1323→{vost_hallmark}
  1324→from
  1325→{vose_brand_file}
  1326→or
  1327→{vose_registry}
  1328→plus `HEAD`
  1329→* `` — Empty identity field (VOK operations lack heat/pace nesting)
  1330→* `ACTION` — Single-letter operation code
  1331→+
  1332→The
  1333→{vost_hallmark}
  1334→source follows the same resolution as Job Jockey:
  1335→first check `.vvk/vvbf_brand.json` in the target repo; if absent or operation is in the kit forge, use
  1336→{vose_registry}
  1337→plus `HEAD`.
  1338→
  1339→[[vosca_vvb]]
  1340→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
  1341→{vosca_vvb}::
  1342→Voce Viva brand prefix.
  1343→Literal string "vvb" appearing as the first field in the
  1344→{voscf_format}.
  1345→
  1346→=== Action Codes
  1347→
  1348→[[voscaa_allocate]]
  1349→// ⟦axl_voices axo_operation⟧
  1350→{voscaa_allocate}::
  1351→Action code `A`.
  1352→Allocates a new
  1353→{vost_hallmark}
  1354→in the
  1355→{vose_registry}.
  1356→+
  1357→Example:
  1358→+
  1359→----
  1360→vvb:1000::A: allocated hallmark for initial release
  1361→----
  1362→
  1363→[[voscar_release]]
  1364→// ⟦axl_voices axo_operation⟧
  1365→{voscar_release}::
  1366→Action code `R`.
  1367→Creates a
  1368→{vose_parcel}
  1369→and tags the release.
  1370→+
  1371→Example:
  1372→+
  1373→----
  1374→vvb:1000::R: release parcel 1000
  1375→----
  1376→
  1377→**Cross-reference:**
  1378→The
  1379→{vost_hallmark}
  1380→concept is shared with Job Jockey's `jjb:` commit format (documented in JJSA-GallopsData.adoc).
  1381→Both systems use the same resolution logic for sourcing
  1382→{vost_hallmark_s},
  1383→but Job Jockey includes heat/pace identity in the third field while VOK leaves it empty.
  1384→
  1385→
  1386→== Whisper Builder API
  1387→
  1388→The
  1389→{vose_whisper}
  1390→builder provides a fluent interface for
  1391→{vose_kit}
  1392→registration.
  1393→
  1394→=== Required Methods
  1395→
  1396→`Whisper::for_kit(id: &str)` — Create whisper for kit with given
  1397→{vosem_kit_id}.
  1398→
  1399→`.cipher(c: Cipher)` — Set the
  1400→{vost_cipher}
  1401→(symbolic const from `voci_ciphers`).
  1402→
  1403→`.display_name(name: &str)` — Set human-readable name.
  1404→
  1405→`.register(&mut Conclave)` — Finalize and register with
  1406→{vose_conclave}.
  1407→
  1408→=== Optional Methods
  1409→
  1410→`.managed_section(file: &str, tag: &str)` — Add a
  1411→{vose_managed_section}.
  1412→May be called multiple times.
  1413→
  1414→`.concept_model(path: &str)` — Register a
  1415→{vosa_concept_model}
  1416→for acronym indexing.
  1417→May be called multiple times.
  1418→
  1419→=== Validation
  1420→
  1421→Each builder method validates its input immediately.
  1422→Final validation occurs at `.register()`:
  1423→
  1424→* {vosem_kit_id}
  1425→must be valid identifier
  1426→* {vost_cipher}
  1427→must be a valid const from `voci_ciphers`
  1428→* All
  1429→{vose_managed_section}
  1430→files must exist
  1431→* All
  1432→{vosa_concept_model}
  1433→files must exist
  1434→
